nameMeanings.name: Cooke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Cook  .

History: Francis Cooke (died 1663) and his eldest son John were passengers on the Mayflower in 1621; they were joined two years later by Francis's wife and other children. In the words of William Bradford, he lived long enough ‘to see his children's children have children’.

variants.name: Cook
meanings: English: occupational name for a cook, a seller of cooked meats, or a keeper of an eating house, from Middle English cok, coke, cook, couk, cuk(e) (Old English cōc) ‘cook’ or ‘seller of cooked foods’. See also Kew .
Irish and Scottish: usually identical in origin with the English name (see 1 above), but in some cases a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cúg ‘son of Hugo’ (see McCook ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘cook’, such as German and Jewish Koch , Dutch Kook , Polish Kucharz and Kucharczyk , Slovenian and Croatian Kuhar , North German Kuk .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Richmond Becomes the Capital
historicEvents.description: On April 18, 1780 Richmond became the capital of Virginia. It was the temporary capital from 1780-1788.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History- History of Richmond Virginia

historicEvents.description: The American Revolution ended with the surrender of Cornwallis at Yorktown Virginia.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.
Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: Tennessee Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: On June 1, 1796, Tennessee became the 16th state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Tennessee Secretart of State-From Territory to State

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Cooke was born in 1760, in Gloucester, Gloucester, Virginia, United States. He married Susan Cousins on 1 July 1782, in Frederick, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in 1799, in Grainger, Tennessee, United States, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Cooke est né en 1760, à Gloucester, Gloucester, Virginie, États-Unis. Il a épousé Susan Cousins le 1 juillet 1782, à Frederick, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1799, à Grainger, Tennessee, États-Unis, à l’âge de 39 ans.
